data.title: Here's How Much You Would Have Made Owning Goldman Sachs Group Stock In The Last 10 Years
data.description:
Goldman Sachs Group (NYSE:GS) has outperformed the market over the past 10 years by 5.69% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 18.82%. Currently, Goldman Sachs Group has a market capitalization of $275.01 billion.

Buying $100 In GS: If an investor had bought $100 of GS stock 10 years ago, it would be worth $551.89 today based on a price of $932.25 for GS at the time of writing.

Goldman Sachs Group's Performance Over Last 10 Years

Finally -- what's the point of all this? The key insight to take from this article is to note how much of a difference compounded returns can make in your cash growth over a period of time.

data.title: Transcript: Baker Hughes Q1 2026 Earnings Conference Call
data.description:
Baker Hughes (NASDAQ:BKR) released first-quarter financial results and hosted an earnings call on Friday. Read the complete transcript below.

This content is powered by Benzinga APIs. For comprehensive financial data and transcripts, visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/.

View the webcast at https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/114619532

Summary

Baker Hughes Co reported strong financial performance in Q1 2026, with adjusted EBITDA at $1.16 billion, exceeding guidance, and a 13% increase in adjusted earnings per share compared to the previous year.

The company achieved record IET orders of $4.9 billion, with significant contributions from power systems, LNG, and gas infrastructure, marking the third consecutive quarter of strong orders.

Baker Hughes Co provided a cautious outlook due to geopolitical uncertainties in the Middle East, expecting a modest impact on IET but a more significant impact on OFSE due to logistical challenges.

Strategic initiatives included the divestiture of Waygate Technologies and a focus on closing the chart transaction to enhance industrial portfolio expansion.

Management emphasized the importance of energy security and diversification, anticipating increased upstream investment and infrastructure development, which positions the company favorably for future growth.

Full Transcript

OPERATOR

Good day, ladies and gentlemen and welcome to The Baker Hughes Company first quarter 2026 earnings call. At this time, all participants are in a listen only mode. Later we will conduct a question and answer session and instructions will follow at that time. As a reminder, this conference call is being recorded. I would now like to introduce your host for today's call, Mr. Chase Mullyhill, Vice President of Investor Relations. Sir, you may begin.

Chase Mullyhill (Vice President of Investor Relations)

Lorenzo Simonelli (Chairman and CEO)

Ahmed Moghul (Chief Financial Officer)

Lorenzo Simonelli (Chairman and CEO)

OPERATOR

We can now open the call for questions. Thank you. If you would like to ask a question, please press Star one on your telephone keypad to raise your hand and join the queue. And if you would like to withdraw that question again, press Star one. We ask that you please limit yourself to one question. Your first question comes from Arun Jaram with JP Morgan. Please go ahead.

Arun Jaram (Equity Analyst at JP Morgan)

Lorenzo Simonelli (Chairman and CEO)

OPERATOR

Your next question comes from the line of Scott Gruber with Citi. Please go ahead.

Scott Gruber (Equity Analyst at Citi)

Ahmed

Scott Gruber (Equity Analyst at Citi)

It does. Thank you. I'll turn it back. Thank you. Thanks.

OPERATOR

Your next question comes from the line of James west with Melius Research. Please go ahead.

James West (Equity Analyst at Melius Research)

Ahmed

James West (Equity Analyst at Melius Research)

That very helpful. Yeah, thanks Abbott. Super. Thanks, James.

OPERATOR

Your next question comes from the line of David Anderson with Barclays. Please go ahead.

David Anderson (Equity Analyst at Barclays)

Lorenzo Simonelli (Chairman and CEO)

David Anderson (Equity Analyst at Barclays)

Clearly a lot upside in power. Thanks a lot Lorenzo. Appreciate it.

OPERATOR

Your next question comes from the line of Stephen Gangaro with Stifel. Please go ahead.

Stephen Gangaro (Equity Analyst at Stifel)

Lorenzo Simonelli (Chairman and CEO)

Stephen Gangaro (Equity Analyst at Stifel)

So hopefully, Stephen, that gives you a little bit of color on how we think about the portfolio. That's great detail. Thank you. Super. Thanks.

OPERATOR

Your next question comes from the line of Sir Rob Pant with Bank of America. Please go ahead.

Rob Pant

Lorenzo Simonelli (Chairman and CEO)

OPERATOR

And that's all the time we had for questions today. I will hand you back to Mr. Lorenzo Simonelli, chairman and chief executive officer, to conclude the call.

Lorenzo Simonelli (Chairman and CEO)

Thank you to everyone for taking the time to join our earnings call today, and I look forward to speaking with you all again soon. Operator, you may now close out the call.

data.title: Full Transcript: Chemed Q1 2026 Earnings Call
data.description:
On Friday, Chemed (NYSE:CHE) discussed first-quarter financial results during its earnings call. The full transcript is provided below.

Benzinga APIs provide real-time access to earnings call transcripts and financial data. Visit https://www.benzinga.com/apis/ to learn more.

The full earnings call is available at https://edge.media-server.com/mmc/p/o65jro38/

Summary

Chemed Corp reported higher than expected revenue growth and EBITDA margins for its VITAS division, driven by a balanced patient mix and lower hospice labor costs.

The Roto Rooter segment saw signs of improvement with increased residential plumbing and sewer revenue, despite facing weather-related disruptions and increased marketing costs.

The company revised its full-year 2026 guidance upward for VITAS, predicting higher ADC growth and revenue, while Roto Rooter's guidance remains unchanged due to persistent marketing cost challenges.

Chemed Corp acquired Roto Rooter franchises in San Francisco and Fort Worth, contributing to revenue growth and aligning with its strategy to boost market share.

Management expressed confidence in resolving past challenges and achieving sustainable growth, highlighting ongoing strategic initiatives and market expansion plans.

Full Transcript

OPERATOR

Holly Schmidt (Assistant Controller)

Kevin McNamara (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Mike Woodsman (Chief Financial Officer)

Joel Worley (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Kevin McNamara (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Thank you, Joel. I will now open this teleconference to questions.

OPERATOR

Thank you. As a reminder to ask a question, you need to press Star 11 on your telephone and wait for a name to be announced. To withdraw your question, please press star 11 again. Please stand by while we compile the Q and A roster. One moment for our first question. First question comes from the lineup. Brian Tanquillet from Jefferies. Your line is open.

Megan Holtom

Mike Woodsman (Chief Financial Officer)

Joel Worley (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Megan Holtom

Thank you. And then on the Roto rooter side, it looked like you guys had some additional marketing expense in the quarter. Is that now the right run rate going forward? And you started seeing some pressure on the customers this time last year, given the macro backdrop. And we're facing a similar headwind in terms of the economy right now and whatnot. So are you seeing that similar trends as we're a month into Q2 now?

Kevin McNamara (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Mike Woodsman (Chief Financial Officer)

Kevin McNamara (President and Chief Executive Officer)

It's real early in Q2. I think things continue to progress the way we expect them to. Got it.

Megan Holtom

Thank you guys.

Kevin McNamara (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Thanks, Megan.

OPERATOR

Thank you. One moment for our next question. Next question will come from the line of Joanna Gadrick from Bank of America. Your line is open.

Joanna Gadrick

Mike Woodsman (Chief Financial Officer)

Joanna Gadrick

Thanks for that. And if I may, so, on the collection rate, did I hear right? I guess maybe there was some improvement, but I guess you've been not expecting Q2, so I guess I just want to make sure you're still expecting to improve collection by 4 to 6 million dollars for the year.

Mike Woodsman (Chief Financial Officer)

Yeah, we were slightly better than that, than our expectations in the first quarter. Having said that, part of that obviously comes from the idea that we're billing less per job. So we anticipate both of those things improving as we finalize the centralization and those centralized employees get more experience and we can bring up the revenue per job while still maintaining a higher collection rate.

Joanna Gadrick

That makes sense. And with these acquisitions, like you mentioned, they usually just come with somewhat lower margins. Obviously, Accretive is still making money and the goal is to improve over time. Do you anticipate doing more of these this year and are there some maybe other assets you would consider acquiring for that business?

Kevin McNamara (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Joanna Gadrick

And I guess any progress. You mentioned you're making some traction with Google Argos and stuff, and I guess you had this new SEO partner, so can you give us an update there?

Kevin McNamara (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Joanna Gadrick

Okay, thank you for that. And switching to VDAs then. I just want to make sure, because we hear other companies calling out the weather disruption in healthcare services, so sounds like it wasn't material because you guys didn't call it out.

Joel Worley (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Joanna Gadrick

Mike Woodsman (Chief Financial Officer)

27 at a very high level, very high level. We think that the revenue, the rate increase might be slightly lower in Florida than the national average. But we're still, you know, we're still crunching the numbers and we don't have the details. They don't come out until sometime in the summer.

Joel Worley (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Yeah. Keeping in mind that is the proposed wage rule, we're still in the comment period and a final wage rule typically is not put into place until the late part of the third quarter,

Joanna Gadrick

Joel Worley (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Kevin McNamara (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Joanna Gadrick

So should I read this as you don't think like the new scoring system that they proposed is going to be finalized. There were some other things that CMS proposed but they never really enforced it because they couldn't really figure out how they're going to measure things. So is it a similar situation with this particular one?

Joel Worley (President and Chief Executive Officer)

We want to make sure that the criteria and the algorithms used to evaluate the scoring with SSVI makes sense and is accurate and does not include data that is been infiltrated by fraudulent claims processing from these providers. They've got to be able to filter all of that out and focus on legitimate providers and the measurement of the care and services provide provided from those.

Joanna Gadrick

Joel Worley (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Kevin McNamara (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Joanna, let me thank.

Joanna Gadrick

Sure, go ahead.

Kevin McNamara (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Joanna Gadrick

Exactly. All right, thank you so much for additional car and for taking the question.

Kevin McNamara (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Thanks, Joanna.

OPERATOR

Thank you. Once again, that's Star114 question. Our next question comes to Michael Murray from RVC Capital Markets. Your line is open.

Michael Murray

Hey, thanks for taking my question for VTAs. I think you're probably seeing a higher mix of admissions from hospitals in your new Florida markets. Just given your current cap situation in this state, how are you thinking about community based admissions in these markets?

Joel Worley (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Kevin McNamara (President and Chief Executive Officer)

And Michael, keep in mind that with the. You specifically mentioned the new starts. When we have a new start, there is not by definition an existing base of long stay patients. So regardless of where in the new starts, only regardless of where the preadmission location is, for some period of time, they're all short stay patients. Because we don't have a base of existing long stay patients.

Michael Murray

You have legacy patients from past government plan years. Right.

Kevin McNamara (President and Chief Executive Officer)

So when you're talking specifically about the new starts, think of them all as short stay patients for at least a period of time.

Michael Murray

Six months. Okay. And then, you know, these new markets are pretty sizable. How should we think about the volume opportunity longer term? You know, what's your typical market share in Florida and how should we think about the range markets?

Kevin McNamara (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Michael Murray

All right, and then just one more on Roto Rooter. So just wanted to get a sense what your current mix of paid leads versus organic leads is and what are your expectations? Expectations embedded in your guidance?

Mike Woodsman (Chief Financial Officer)

Paid leads are roughly 53 to 54% of our total leads at the moment. We anticipate that mix to continue. We have not anticipated a deterioration or a significant improvement. And that's why again, we took our guidance and added $3 million of additional marketing costs, marketing costs for the rest of the year. But we don't, we haven't projected a significant deterioration in that from the first quarter.

Kevin McNamara (President and Chief Executive Officer)

OPERATOR

Thank you. I'm not showing any further questions in the queue. I'd like to turn it back over to Kevin for any closing remarks.

Kevin McNamara (President and Chief Executive Officer)

Thank you, everyone. We're happy. We had what we thought was a good quarter, excellent quarter at Vitas and some good trends at both companies. And we look forward to reporting on our results for the current quarter in due course. Thank you very much.

data.title: What's Going On With Micron Technology Stock Friday?
data.description:
AI Memory Shortage Sparking ‘Red-Hot’ Rally

On Thursday, CNBC’s Jim Cramer said Micron Technology is benefiting from a surge in artificial intelligence demand that has outpaced global memory supply.

He noted that AI workloads require massive memory capacity, while the industry remains constrained, creating a supply shortage that is driving prices and lifting stocks.

Cramer said these stocks keep climbing because aggressive buyers continue placing large orders at higher prices. He described the group as driven by persistent investor demand and that the rally is still in its early stages.

Micron Technology Recent Analyst Moves

The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $517.08. Recent analyst moves include:

  • UBS: Buy (Raises Target to $535.00) (April 8)
  • Citigroup: Buy (Lowers Target to $425.00) (March 31)
  • JP Morgan: Overweight (Raises Target to $550.00) (March 19)

Technical Analysis

Micron is pressing the top of its 52-week range after setting a new high in April, which keeps the longer-term trend pointed up. The stock is trading 21.8% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 37% above its 100-day SMA at $502.69, a setup that leans toward strong short- and intermediate-term control by buyers.

The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum measure, is bullish with the MACD line above the signal line, which is consistent with upside momentum staying in place. When MACD stays above its signal, it usually means pullbacks are getting bought faster than they’re sold.

  • Key Resistance: $493.50 — prior 52-week high area that can act like a “speed bump” on breakouts.
  • Key Support: $412.50 — near the 20-day/50-day SMA zone where dip-buyers often defend trend.

Benzinga Edge Rankings

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for Micron Technology, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

The Verdict: Micron Technology’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a momentum-driven story with strong growth and quality backing it. With Value closer to neutral, the setup reads less like a bargain play and more like a trend-and-execution narrative.

Micron Technology Top ETF Exposure

Significance: Because MU carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

Price Action

MU Stock Price Activity: Micron Technology shares were up 4.36% at $502.73 at the time of publication on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

data.title: Can AuthID's Quantum-Resistant PrivacyKey Spark A Breakout?
data.description:
The stock is moving higher following the launch of its quantum-resistant PrivacyKey biometric authentication platform, which aims to eliminate threats posed by quantum computing and enhance identity verification security.

The biometric authentication firm said that PrivacyKey integrates NIST post-quantum standards with multi-party computation (MPC) to secure cryptographic keys. It positions the firm as a solid biometric authentication platform built to withstand future quantum threats.

ServiceNow Identity Integration

AuthID said the integration allows organizations to verify and authenticate caller identities within ServiceNow-powered contact centers to help protect accounts, reduce fraud and enhance trust. The company said the integration supports both consumer and workforce identity use cases and is available through the ServiceNow Store.

Under the integration, AuthID’s Proof, Verified and PrivacyKey products connect with ServiceNow through UI Actions, allowing contact center agents to send SMS or email prompts to users to verify their identity before making account changes or completing high-risk transactions.

The broader market saw gains on Thursday, with the Technology sector rising 1.82%. The stock’s increase comes as the Technology sector is currently ranked as the best performing sector, indicating that authID is moving in line with broader positive trends.

Technical Analysis

AuthID is trading at $1.27, which is 8.3% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) of $1.17, suggesting a short-term bullish trend. However, it remains 9.1% below its 50-day SMA of $1.40 and 4.9% below its 100-day SMA of $1.34, indicating some intermediate-term weakness.

The stock has declined 83.93% over the past 12 months, reflecting a challenging year for the company. Currently, it is positioned above its 52-week low of 84 cents but well below its high of $9.58, which suggests it is still recovering from a prolonged downturn.

  • Key Resistance: $1.40 — This level has historically been a barrier for upward movement.
  • Key Support: $1.00 — A critical level where buying interest may emerge.

Sector Performance

AuthID is currently underperforming the broader technology sector, lagging by 9.12 percentage points despite a 1.82% gain on Thursday, while the sector itself has risen 13.95% over the past month. This suggests the stock is not fully benefiting from the strong overall tech rally and may be facing company-specific headwinds.

The company specializes in biometric identity verification for enterprises, helping confirm user identities at login and transaction points through its PrivacyKey platform, which authenticates users without storing biometric data.

Its focus on fraud prevention and advanced security, including resilience against emerging threats like quantum computing, positions it within a high-demand cybersecurity niche even as it trails broader sector performance.

Earnings & Analyst Outlook

Looking further out, the next major catalyst for the stock arrives with the May 12, 2026 (estimated) earnings report.

  • EPS Estimate: 40 cents
  • Revenue Estimate: 30 cents Million (Down from 40 cents Million YoY)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a N/A Rating. No recent analyst actions available.

Price Action

AUID Stock Price Activity: AuthID shares were up 4.96% at $1.27 during premarket trading on Friday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock
